claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're looking to claim Child Benefit for Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now, could you please tell me: do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next, could you tell me about your living arrangements with Alex? Do you live with Alex, or does Alex live elsewhere?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - they live elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex lives elsewhere, I have a couple of follow-up questions.

Could you tell me who Alex currently lives with? For example, is it a friend, relative, foster carer, or someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent - my ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Do you contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep? This could include things like paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or contributing to their accommodation costs.

If so, is the amount you contribute at least equal to the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week for a first child)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help with things like clothes and other expenses. I contribute £30 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know that you contribute £30 per week, which is above the Child Benefit rate.

One more important question: is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? For example, is your ex-partner (who Alex lives with) already receiving Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, my ex-partner isn't claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Nobody else is getting it for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's very helpful.

Just one final question to make sure I have all the information: how long has Alex been living with your ex-partner? Has it been more than 8 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been quite a while now - definitely more than a couple of months.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

- ✅ **Alex's age**: At 8 years old, Alex is under 16 and meets the age requirement
- ✅ **You live in the UK**: This is a key eligibility requirement
- ✅ **Financial contributions**: Although Alex doesn't live with you, you contribute £30 per week towards their upkeep (clothes and other expenses), which is above the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for a first child
- ✅ **No competing claims**: No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Under the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after the child, even if the child doesn't live with you.

**Important things to note:**
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so if your ex-partner decides to claim in the future, you would need to agree between yourselves who should receive it
- If you or a partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay some of it back through a tax charge

You can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Although Alex does not live with the claimant (they live with the other parent), the claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (for things like clothes and other expenses). This amount exceeds the Child Benefit rate of £25.60 per week for a first child. Under the rules, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after the child. Additionally, no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since the claimant lives in the UK and is making sufficient contributions to Alex's upkeep, they meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex (age 8), who lives with the other parent. Although the child doesn't live with them, the claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit threshold of £25.60 per week. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
